title: Flow of Exception Propagation using the 'throw' Keyword
description: A flowchart illustrating how an exception is explicitly created and thrown within a method, and how it propagates up the call stack until it is caught by a matching handler.
relationships: Method executes until it reaches a 'throw' statement
'throw' creates an Exception object and immediately halts normal execution
The exception propagates up the call stack, exiting each method
A 'catch' block with a matching exception type handles it, or the program terminates if uncaught
